inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. B 12 of 1942-43.)
Kaḍumūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A stone pillar set up in front of the Āñjanēyasvāmi temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1457 (current) Jaya, Vaiśākha śu. 10, Thursday, corresponding
to 15348 A.D., April 23.

Records the establishment of a pillar (Diva-kambhamu) at the temple of Hanumanta by
Abuḷa-Tirumalayyagāru son of Annaṁgāru of Bhōjanapalli Kadamūru.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. B 23 of 1942-43.)
Pagiḍyāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Kārttīka śu. 12, corresponding to 1555 A.D.,
October 27, Sunday.

It registers a gift of one Varāha per year to the god Vīranna of Payiḍāla situated in
the Mosalimaḍugu-sīma by Sāḷuva Timmaya and another person (his name is missing in the
record) for the merit of the twenty four families of their Dommari community.

l.te: ka śu 12 lu śrīmaṁnmahārājādhirājaparamēśvara śrī vī-

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. B 24 of 1942-43.)
Pagiḍyāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the primary school.

This is dated in Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Śrāvaṇa ba. 5, corresponding to 1555 A.D.,
August 7, Wednesday.

It registers the grant made by the three Vipravinōdins Anaṁtayya, Govindayya and
mādhavayya, of a portion of their annual incomes (Varttana) bestowed upon their community
by the Mahājanas of the place to god Kēśava Perumāḷ of Payiṇḍālu alias Bhāvanārāyaṇapuram
which was included in the Mosalimaḍugu-sīma and belonged to Kandāḷa Śrīraṅgāchārya.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. B 25 of 1942-43.)
Pagiḍyāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Āñjanēyasvāmi temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1439, Īśvara, Jyēshṭha ba. 30, Friday, Solar eclipse,
corresponding to 1517 A.D., June 19.

It records that Yenumula Timmānāyanigāru restored the lands as sarvamānya gift to the
gods at Paiṁḍāla by exempting the assessment before god Mallikārjuna at Bejavāḍa, on the
banks of the Kṛishna, for the merit of Kṛishnarāya-Mahārāya and his own preceptor and
parents.

l.te: ṁvatsara jēṣṭa ba 30 śu śrīmatu yenumula
la dhānyālu kritaṁ koṁdaru rājulu dharmamudappi pū-
rikini tama tallikini tamakuṁnu puṁṇyaṁgānu pu-

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. B 26 of 1942-43.)
Paipālem, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Āñjanēyasvāmi temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Kārttīka śu. 12, corresponding to 1547 A.D.,
October 25, Tuesday.

It registers the grant of the village Vūḍu-Guḍipāḍu, situated in the Kandanavrōli-sīma as
sarvamānya inclusive of all the privileges such as Kāṇika, Veṭivēmula, Kaṭapaṁgalu, etc., and the
suṁka-sthāvaram as in vogue in the days of the Daṇā-yani Basuvappa to Rāvuladēvuni Akrāju
by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju-Veṅkaṭādri Ayyadēva-Mahārāju for the merit of his father
Śriraṅgarāju, on the asuspicious occasion of Utthāna-dvādaśī in the presence of the god Viṭhala.

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. B 27 of 1942-43.)
Pālamaṟṟi, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone built in to the ceiling of the Kēśasvāmi temple.

This is dated only in the cyclic year Krōdhi, Jyēśṭha śu. 15. However on the basis of
Palaeography, it may be assigned to the 17th century A.D.,

It registers a gift of land for the maintenance of a perpetual lamp to the god
Chennarāya at Pālamaṟṟi.

l.te: krōdhi saṁhvatsa[ra*] jēṣṭa śu 15 lu • • • • • kalivariṣaṁttalaya-

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. B 28 of 1942-43.)
Pārumañchāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A pillar in the Chennakēśavasvāmi temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Maghā śu. 15, corresponding to 1515 A.D., January
30, Tuesday.

It records the construction of a temple (nagaru) to god Chennakēśava at Pārumaṁchāla
by Sāṁtayya, son of Savaram Rāminēḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. B 29 of 1942-43.)
Pārumañchāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Broken stone lying in a field known as Turakapalle Pāṭimīndichēnu, about 6 km from
the village.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 18th century, on the basis of palaeography.

It states that the land situated to the north of the boundary of Turakalapalle field is
assigned to the god Allāḍanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. B 30 of 1942-43.)
Pārumañchāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stones built in to the wall of a well known as Peddabāvī.

This is dated Śaka 1746, Tāraṇa, Māgha ba. 12, corresponding to 1825 A.D., February
14, Monday.
It mentions the construction of a well by Chaṁnāreḍḍi, son of Kēśava-reḍḍi-Vīrāreḍḍi.

l.te: nēṭi tāraṇa nāma saṁ || māgha ba 12

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. B 31 of 1942-43.)
Saṅgamēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the ghāṭ near the western gate of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
This is dated Heijra 1153, Rudhirōdgāri.

It states that Heijra Mahammadu Sāhebulagāru exempted the toll on the pilgrims
arriving for an auspicious bath in the river Kṛishnā on the occasion of Kanyā-Bhāgīrathi.

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. B 34 of 1942-43.)
Saṅgamēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Pillar in the maṇḍapa to the right of the entrance of the central shrine in the
Saṅgamēśvara temple.
This undated record may be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.
It mentions Sāḷva, Aḍivi Basavarāju Pārthasārathi Nāyuḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. B 35 of 1942-43.)
Saṅgamēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
This undated record may be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.
It mentions the obeisance of the mason Guruvappa, son of Dāmaḍugu Satra Battuḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. B 36 of 1942-43.)
Saṅgamēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa to the left of the northern entrance of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
This undated record may be assigned to the 13th century on the basis of Palaeography.
It states that the worshipping figure represents Goraya.

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. B 36 of 1942-43.)
Saṅgamēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Pillar in the maṇḍapa to the left of the northern entrance (below) of the Saṅgamēśvara
temple.
This undated record may be assigned to the 13th century on the basis of palaeography.

It states that Vibhūti Gaura, born at Māchirājapalli near Oruṁgal residing on the top of
the Śrī-giri, a servant of the house of Śrī Paṇḍitārādhya solicits god's protection.

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. B 37 of 1942-43.)
Sātānikōṭa, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Boulder in a field about 3 km away from the village.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 10th century, on the basis of paleography.
It reads Śrī-Īśaraṉṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. B 39 of 1942-43.)
Siddhēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Two sides (other) of a pillar built into the platform of the Āshur-khāna.

This is dated Śaka 1482, Raudri, Āshāḍha śu. 11, Wednesday, corresponding to 1560
A.D. July 3.

Damaged. It seems to register some gift to god Nimirti Saṅgamēśvara by Chennama
Kṛishṇama Nāyaniṁgā, son of Rānēḍu and grandson of Mudulagurti Likinēḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. B 40 of 1942-43.)
Siddhēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Another pillar built into the platform of the Āshur-khāna.

It registers a gift of some Vṛittis of land in two villages made by Māmala Mallaya, a
feudatory of the king to god Visavanātha at Siddhēśvaram whose temple was constructed in the
name of Viśvēśvara Śivadēva, the Rājaguru of Gaṇapatidēva Mahārāju. The management of the
endowed land was handed over to Pōchirāju Veṅgana Pegaḍa, the agent of Vidyāśivadēva, who
was the sthānapati of Saṁgamēśvara and Bhāskarakshētra.

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. B 41 of 1942-43.)
Siddhēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A broken pillar lying in the compound of the Siddhēśvara temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 12th century, on the basis of internal
evidence.

Fragmentary. It seems to record an endowment of some Vṛittis of land after purchase
by Kannaya and Mallaya for 100 gadyāṇas.

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. B 42 of 1942-43.)
Siddhēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the southern gōpura outside the Mallikārjuna temple.

Damaged and worn out. It seems to register some gift by a certain Nāyanigāru (name
not clear) of Vēlūru for the merit of his father. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. B 43 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
slab set up on the paved way leading from the souther gōpura of the Mallikārjuna to
the Cheñchu colony.

This is dated Śaka 1327, Pārtiva, Chaitra ba. 10, Wednesday, corresponding to 1405 A.D.,
March 26. However the week day is Thursday and not Wednesday.

It states that Kāṭmareḍḍi Vēmireḍḍi constructed the stone steps leading to Srikanṭhēśvara
for the spiritual merit of Komaragirireḍḍi and his queen Ammaṅ-gāru. Komaragirireḍḍi bears
the epithets, Jaganoppagaṇḍa and Gajapati-vibhāḷa among others.

inscription.head:
No. 475.
(A.R. No. B 45 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar set up near the southern gōpura inside the Mallikārjuna temple.

This is dated Śaka 1322, Vikrama, Adhika Chaitra ba. 15, Solar eclipse, corresponding
to 1400 A.D., April 1 Friday.

Fragmentary. The portion of the inscription containing the details of the grant etc. is
broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 476.
(A.R. No. B 46 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of the Annapūrṇa-satram inside the Mallikārjuna temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 15th-16th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

Damaged and worn out. It records the services rendered by Gumṭipalle Mutta-rāju who
is described as one interested in performing the sixteen mahādānas and who has presented
golden balls to god Śrī Girinātha.

inscription.head:
No. 477.
(A.R. No. B 47 of 1942-43.)
Śriśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Nandi pillar set up in the courtyard to the south of the Nandi-maṇḍapa inside the
Mallikārjuna temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
It records the offering of a lamp to the god Mallikārjuna by Gaṁgajiya and Vīramma.

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. B 49 of 1942-43.)
Śriśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Four sides of a broken stone pillar set up in the Mallikārjuna temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 14th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
Badly damaged and worn out. It mentions Śrīparvata and Śrī Mallikārjuna.

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. B 50 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Pillar built into the wall of the southern entrance to the inner prākāra of the
Mallikārjuna temple.

This is dated Śaka 1525, (expired) Śōbhakṛit, Bhādrapada śu. 15, corresponding to 1602
A.D., August 22, Sunday.

Records the construction of some shrines and halls probably in the Mallikārjuna temple
by Haṇḍe Ḍēmappa Nāyaniṅgāru.

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(A.R. No. B 51 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A Nandi pillar set up near the Mallikārjuna guṇḍam, inside the Mallikārjuna temple.

This is dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Jyēshṭha ba. 15, Thursday, corresponding to 1534 A.D.,
June 11.
Damaged. It mentions only certain individual Varadaiah.

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. B 52 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailam, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Nandi pillar (another) set up near the Mallikārjuna guṇḍam, inside the Mallikārjuna
temple.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of palaeography.
Badly damaged. It mentions Basvaya.

inscription.head:
No. 482.
(A.R. No. B 53 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailam, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Nandi pillar (another) set up near the Mallikārjuna guṇḍam, inside the Mallikārjuna
temple.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of palaeography.
Badly damaged. It reads Goruvunāthaṁ.

inscription.head:
No. 483.
(A.R. No. B 54 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A mutilated slab lying in the Mallikārjuna guṇḍam, inside the Mallikārjuna temple.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 13th century, on the basis of palaeography.

Damaged and fragmentary. It records the founding of a village called Gaṇapavuram in
the name of his mother and making a gift of it to god Śrīgirinātha (i.e., Mallikārjuna at
Śrīśailam) by the general Kāchaya Reḍḍi who bore the epithet, Mahārāyasthapanā-chārya (the
establisher of the great king) and who is said to have vanquished the Muhammadan ruler that
had penetrated into the Telugu country after conquering the Gauḷa, Gūrjara, Māḷava,
Mahārāshṭra and other provinces. He is stated to be the son of Mailaya Reḍḍi who placed on a
firm footing the Kākita throne (Piṭha). A number of food-items have been mentioned the levies
on which items was transferred to the village authorities.

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. B 55 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Broken pillar set up in the compound of a Śiva temple to the west of land with in the
Prākāra of the Mallikārjuna temple.

This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 14th century, on the basis of the internal
evidence of the record.

It records the installation of a liṅga named Trayaṁbaka for the prosperity of his family
by Anantarasa son of Gōparasa Daṁḍanātha and grandson of Baichaya Daṇḍanātha who was
the hereditary minister of king Bukka. Gōparasa is stated to be the minister of Harihara.

l.te: nāṁgē kōśa gaja turaga śrīratna dē-

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. B 56 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Nandi pillar set up near the northern gōpura out side the Mallikārjuna temple.

This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 16th-17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
It states the erection of a lamp-post (Dīpa-kamba) by a person named Kalyāṇam Ārya.

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. B 57 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Another Nandi pillar set up near the northern gōpura out side the Mallikārjuna temple.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of palaeography.

It mentions the name of Parvataya-Nāgaya, son of the mason Māchi Batu of
Dēvarakoṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 487.
(A.R. No. B 58 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Third Nandi pillar set up near the northern gōpura outside the Mallikārjuna temple.

This is dated Śaka 1449, Vyaya, Āshāḍa śu. 14, Monday. However the details are
irregular.

Damaged. It seems to register a gift of land to the god Mallikārjuna for offerings etc.
It mentions Koṁḍamarasayya, Parvatayya and Siddhaya.

inscription.head:
No. 488.
(A.R. No. B 59 of 1942-43.)
Śrīśailaṁ, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying outside the northern prākāra wall of the Mallikārjuna temple.
This is dated Śaka 1089 (expressed in chronogram), Āshāḍa śu. 5, corresponding to 1167 A.D.

Damaged and fragmentary. It records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the god
Mallikārjuna of Śrīśaila by Vīra-Rājendrachōḷa. it also gives a string of birudas of the chief,
among which may be noted the title denoting his lordship over the town Indulūru in Pākanaḍu
(Pākanāṁṭi Yindulūri-Purānganāvallabha).

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(A.R. No. B 63 of 1942-43.)
Tartūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of the Kēśavasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1650, Kīlaka, Jyēshṭa śu. 5, corresponding to 1728 A.D., June 1,
Saturday.

It states the construction of a Dharma-maṇḍapam by Huḍēdārs. It also refers to gōmāji-
śēva (service instituted by gōmāji) in the same year, but on Śu. 10.

l.te: kīlaka nāma saṁva[tsa]cara jēṣṭa śudha deśami 10 lu

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(A.R. No. B 65 of 1942-43.)
Tartūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab setup near Pīrala Chāvadi.

This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Bhādrapada śu. 11, corresponding to 1544 A.D.,
August 28, Thursday.

Damaged. It seems to register a gift of land to the temples of Mallikārjuna,
Kaṁnīśvara, Vīrabhadradēva and others by Koṁḍōju-ayyadēva-Mahārāju, Malrāju ayyadēva-
Mahārāju and mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōbbūri Narasarājayya from their amaram fields in the
Kamdanavōli-sīma.

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(A.R. No. B 66 of 1942-43.)
Vaḍḍamānu, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the well called Kāḍi-bāvi.
This is dated Śaka 1476, Rudhirōdgāri, Margasira ba. 10. Irregular.

Damaged and badly wornout. It seems to be a grant. The details of the donee and
donor are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. B 67 of 1942-43.)
Vaḍḍamānu, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Yallamma temple.

This is dated Śaka 1467, (current), Krōdhi, Chaitra ba. 1. corresponding to 1545 A.D.
March 24, Sunday.

Badly damaged and worn out. It mentions a mahāmaṇḍalēśvara and also king
Sadāśivarāya.

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(A.R. No. B 99 of 1942-43.)
Turiñjikuppam, Polur Taluk, North Arcot District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Two stones set up by the side of Śiva temple in ruins.

This is dated Śaka 1432 (expressed by a chronogram), Śu. . may correspond to 1510
A.D.

Damaged. It records that Śiva Dīkshita, son of Mallamāmba who was learned and had
performed many makhas obtained Kiñjalikuppam from the king and after construction of two
tanks, gave it to four of his relatives on conditions that they should not bear any ill-will
amongst themselves, nor mingle matrimonially with other castes, should be ardent in the study
of the Vēdas and Śāstras and should worship only gods like Vishṇu or Īśvara and not evil
deities.

inscription.head:
No. 494.
(A.R. No. B 66 of 1942-43.)
Dharanivarāhapuram, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the jungle to the south of the Kaṇṇimār (Saptamātris) shrine.

This is dated in the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Bhādrapada ba. 15. However, on the basis of
palaeography, it may be assigned to the 17th century.

It records the tax (in kind) levied on grain harvested from the lands watered by the
tank at Chirutaṇi, for its maintenance, by Baṁgāru Nāyaka, who was administering Chirutaṇi-
sīma which was given over for administration by the king to Tūpakula Kṛishnappa-Nāyaka
(ruler of Gingee).

l.te: svasti śrī tāraṇa saṁvatsara bhādrapada ba 15 lu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 495.
(A.R. No. B 116 of 1942-43.)
Velangeri, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the lake bund adjoining the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 18th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states the construction of a tank by Rāja Kāmarāja Bahadūr at the instance of
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaṭhāri Sāḷva Bommarāju Bahadūr.

inscription.head:
No. 496.
(A.R. No. B 122 of 1942-43.)
Śatrunjayapuram or Śrīgirikuppam, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the lake-bund close to the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 18th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states the construction of a tank by Rāja Kāverrāja Bahadūr at the instance of
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaṭāri Sāḷva Bommarāju Bahadur. (cf. No. 495).

inscription.head:
No. 497.
(A.R. No. B 143 of 1942-43.)
Tiruttaṉi, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Stone set upon the tank bund (near Railway line on Arakkonam side).
This undated record may be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.

It states the construction of a tank by Rājā Kāmērrāja Bahdūr at the instance of
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaṭhāri Sāḷva Bommarāju Bahadūr.

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. B 144 of 1942-43.)
Tiruttani, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Stone set upon the tank bund (near Railway line on Arkonam side).
This undated record may be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.

It states the construction of a tank by Rājā Kāmērrāju Bahdūr at the instance of
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaṭhāri Sāḷva Bommarāju Bahadūr.

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. B 145 of 1942-43.)
Paṭṭābhirāmapuram, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Stone set up on the tank bund.
This undated record may be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.

It states the construction of a tank by Rājā Kāmerāju Bahdūr on the orders of
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaṭhāri Sāḷva Bommarāju Bahadūr.

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. B 167 of 1942-43.)
Pūnimāṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Stone set up in a field opposite the Vināyaka shrine.

This is dated only in the cyclic year Saumya, Māgha ba. 5, Thursday. However, the
details are irregular.

It records the death of a hero in fight at Vaidēśirikoṇḍa. In characters of 17th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. B 173 of 1942-43.)
Ārkāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Pedestal of the bronze image of Vighnēśvara in the Chōlēśvara temple.
This undated record may be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.

It refers to a gift made by Kanakāmba, wife of Muddu Veḷḷa-Koṇḍama Nāyaka, son of
Toṇḍmaṇḍala Dhūli Rāghava Nāyaka of Kannappa-gōtra and also states the obeisance of Muddu
Rāmabhadra Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. B 1 of 1943-44.)
Tāḷḷapālem, Machilipatnam Taluk, Krishna District.
inscription.p: Slab forming the threshold of the doorway in the Madanagōpālasvāmi temple.

It records that the Kappa-doḍḍi of the temple was the offering of Jakkā Brammaya
Śāstrulu. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 503.
(A.R. No. B 3 of 1943-44.)
Ōmkāram, Nandyal Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab with the image of Āñjanēya set up under the pipal tree near the tank.

The date is expressed by the chronogram vasu-vārāśi-guṇa-indu in Śaka 1348,
Parābhava, Kārttīka śu. 13, corresponding to 1426 A.D., October 28, Monday.

It records the construction of a tank by Hamsanāyaka, son of Purushōttama and
Chennamāmba.

l.te: svasti śrī vasu vārāśi guṇēṁdu śakavatsarē

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. B 4 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Right pillar in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Chauḍēśvari temple.

The is date Śaka 1492 Pramādi, Āshāḍha śu. 12, Thursday, corresponding to 1570 A.D.,
June 15.

It records the resolution of the Vidvan-mahājanas of Nandavaram on the occasion when
their agrahāra-village was restored to them and they were reinstated in it by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju-Tirumalarājayyadēva Mahārāju, that they would take to the study
of the Vēdas and the Sastras and would not levy Kanyāśulka for marriage of girls in their
community.

inscription.head:
No. 505.
(A.R. No. B 5 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Left pillar in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Chauḍēśvari temple.
This is dated Śaka 1475 Khara, Māgha ba. 12. Irregular.

Damaged. It refers to certain honours and concessions granted to the 360 Ekāngavīras
who were the devotees of the goddess Chauḍēśvari during the time of the pontiff Vīra-Śānta
bhikshāvṛitti-ayya of Śrīśailaṁ to the grant of some varttana by the former to Chākila
Nippamma.

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. B 6 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab paved on the floor in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Chauḍēśvari temple.

This is dated Śaka 1458 (expired), Manmatha, Mārgaśira śu. 15, corresponding to 1535
A.D., December 9, Thursday.

It records that Giriyappa, the sthānapati of Salakarāju Peda-Timmārāju, constructed the
eastern gōpura of the Chauḍēśvari temple and made a gift of a palanquin to the goddess.

l.te: svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śaka varuṣaṁbulu 1458 ·agunēṁṭi
śubhamastu manmata saṁvatsara mā¨rgaśira śu 15 lu [||*] salakarāju

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. B 7 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab fixed (another) on the floor in the Chauḍēśvari temple.

This is dated Śaka 1214 Dundubhi, Śravaṇa śu. 15. However the characters of the
inscription are about the 16th century.

It states that the 360 Ekāngavīras, the devotees of the goddess Chauḍēśvari invoking her
aid, went to help mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Virūparāya-ayadēva Mahārāju, the ruler of Pānugallu, in
order to relieve the siege laid to his fort. After being victorious, they were conferred general
titles by the chief in appreciation of their valour and success. It records an endownment made
by them (the Ekāngavīras) at one ruka per head to the goddess, for making ornaments, daily
and occasional offerings to the deity.

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. B 8 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab paved on the floor in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Chauḍēśvari temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Khara, Māgha ba. 2. However, on the basis of
palaeography it may be assigned to the 17th century.

It registers a gift of land made by the Vidvanmahājanas of Nandavara-agrahāra to the
artisans Yallā-batuḍu of Chinakoṭla and Timmā-batuḍu of Chāṁgalamaṟṟi for rendering service
in the temple. It also specifies the rate of monthly wages in cash and kind to the labourers
who were engaged in the work.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. B 9 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab paved on the floor in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Chauḍēśvari temple.
This is dated Śaka 1475 Khara, Māgha śu. 15, Thursday.

It records that names Pallavali Yallappa and the members of his family who are stated
to be ardent devotees of Chauḍēśvari of Nandavaram.

l.te: khara saṁvatsara māgha śu 15 guruvāraṁ naṁddavaraṁ

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. B 10 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab paved on the floor in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Chauḍēśvari temple.

This is dated Śaka 1548 Kshaya, Chaitra śu. 1, corresponding to 1626 A.D., March 18,
Saturday.

It gives the names of certain individuals who were devotees of the goddess
Chauḍēśvararamma.

l.te: lu 15[4]8 ·agunēṭi kṣaya nāma saṁvvatsara caitra śu 1 lu | [biḷaṁ] jakkaya
komāruḍu caṁddaya caṁddaya komāruḍu pāpaya ·ā pāpaya

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. B 11 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab paved on the floor on the north side of prākāra of the Chauḍēśvari temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 17th-18th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states that Apparāju, son of Teḷḷapūḍi Chiṭṭamarāju worshipped the goddess
Chāmuṇḍēśvari to ward off all evils and attain bliss.

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(A.R. No. B 12 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab on the plateform at the proper right entrance in to the Chauḍēśvari temple.

This is dated Śaka 1707 Visvāvasu, Śrāvaṇa śu. 10, Monday, corresponding to 1785 A.D.,
August 15.

It states that Kambagiri Rāmaya and Sītārāmaya, sons of Dharaṇisubbarāyappa renovated
the temple and installed in it a new idol of Chāmuṇḍēśvari.

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(A.R. No. B 13 of 1943-44.)
Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying near a private house in the village.

The date portion of the record is completely damaged. However, on the basis of
palaeography it may be assigned to the 16th century.

Damaged. It seems to refer to the prohibition of taking or giving kannekalaṁ
(Kanyāśulkaṁ) in marriages among the Vidvanmahajanas of the place. It also records the gift
of four villages (specified) as sarvamānya including the ubhayamārga suṅkam, Siddhayam,
Veṭṭivēmulu etc. for the maintenance of a sastra by Sulire Bālayya.

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(A.R. No. B 14 of 1943-44.)
Tāṭipādu, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Slab on the floor in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple close to the entrance into the
inner shrine.

This is dated in the cyclic year Hemalaṁbi, Śrāvaṇa śu. 1. However, on the basis of
palaeography, it may be assigened to the 17th century A.D.
Incomplete. It refers to an edureḍa (counter agreement) executed by a private individual.

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(A.R. No. B 15 of 1943-44.)
Gaṇapuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up by the side of the temple of Ellamma.

This is dated Śaka 1302 Raudri, Kārttīka śu. 13, Friday, corresponding to 1380 A.D.,
October 12 (Adhika Kārttīka).

It records that Kali Vishṇuṇḍu consecrated a new image of Aṅkāḷadēvi at Rēlapāḍu, the
old image having been broken and endowed lands at Vedurupādu and Tāṭipādu to the priest
Vachebōyini-Tippana for worship of the goddess.

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(A.R. No. B 16 of 1943-44.)
Chēbōlu hamlet of Gaṇapuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying under a margosa tree at the Nāgalakaṭṭa.

This is dated Śaka 1171, (expressed in chronograms) Saumya, Jyēshṭha Sunday, Solar
eclipse, corresponding to 1249 A.D., May 23. However on this day the solar eclipse has not
occurred.

Multilated. It gives the genealogy of the Kākatīyas and registers the grant of the village
Chēṁbrolu to the Brāhmaṇa Padamanābha by the chief Vaṇaga who is said to have ruled over
the Stambhadēśa as subordinate of the king. The donor's ancestry is given.

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(A.R. No. B 17 of 1943-44.)
Kokkerañcha, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab set up near the Lingamayya shrine.

This is dated Śaka 1425, Rudhirōdgāri, Bhādrapada [śu.] 10, Friday, corresponding to
1503 A.D., September 1.

It seems to record a gift of land made to Tirumala Komāra Tātāchārya by Pōtarāju of
Kokerañcha.

l.te: maḍi mēḍi cervu venaka gaṇādhipati vari-

inscription.head:
No. 518.
(A.R. No. B 18 of 1943-44.)
Edurupāḍu, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Boundary stone near a wall called Guṇḍappabhāvi in survey No. 525/B.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
It states that the stone marks the boundary between Kōkeravaṁcha and Edurupāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. B 19 of 1943-44.)
Edurupāḍu, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Boundary stone in survey No. 427, on the way to Gōkavaram.

It states that the stone marks the boundary between Gōkavaram and Vedurupāḍu. In
late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(A.R. No. B 20 of 1943-44.)
Gōkavaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar under a pipal tree in the village.

This is dated Śaka 1460, (expired), Viḷambi, Pushya ba. 30, Sunday, Ardhōdaya,
corresponding to 1539 A.D., January 19.

Slightly damaged. It records that the village Gōṅkavaram situated in the Mosalimaḍugu-
sīma was restored as a sarvamānya gift to the original donees of the village viz.,
Timmappanāyuḍu, son of Mallappa-Nāyuḍu and another by the king.

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. B 22 of 1943-44.)
Rāmāpuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab in the village.

This is dated Śaka 1481, Siddhārthin, Śrāvana śu. 15, corresponding to 1559 A.D., July
19, Wednesday.

Damaged. It registers a grant of land as sarvamānya probably by Gaṅga Vīrappa at the
instance of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju Timmarājayya.

l.te: vaṇa śu 15 lu [||*] śrī madrājā[dhi*]rāja rāja-
vāru | yī dha¨rmā[nku] tapi naḍacina koṁpalu ya-

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. B 23 of 1943-44.)
Pāta-Indrēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Kēśva temple.

This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of palaeography.
It records the obeisance of Peda Timmāreḍḍi, son of Lakkireḍḍi-Vōbuḷreḍḍi to god Kēśava
Perumāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(A.R. No. B 24 of 1943-44.)
Elamalapalle, hamlet of Indrēśvaram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar lying near a multilated image of Āñjanēya near a tank.

This is dated Śaka 1451, Virōdhi, Pushya ba. 10, corresponding to 1529 A.D., December
25.

Damaged. It registers a gift probably of land made for worship of god Mallikārjunadēva
and mentions a devotee of the god who bears a string of epithets such as Shaḍ-darśana
sthāpanāchārya. Śvētāṁbara-talaguṇḍu-gaṇḍa etc. The details regarding the donor and the donee
are not clear.

l.te: lu svasti śrī śrīpa¨rvvata śrī svayaṁbhu-

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. B 20 of 1943-44.)
Kṛishnāpuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Ānanda, Bhādrapada śu. 11. On the basis of
palaeography it may be assigned to the 17th century A.D.

Incomplete. It seems to refer to a gift of land below the tank Lingasamudram at
Timmāpuram by Bejavāḍa Malli-vīrsāni.

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(A.R. No. B 26 of 1943-44.)
Kṛishnāpuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Svabhānu, Kārttīka śu. 15, On the basis of
palaeography it may be assigned to the 17th century A.D.

It states that Pārvatamma of Vijayanagaram made a gift of the beam of the raṅga-maṇṭapa of
the Prasanna Vīrēśvara temple.

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(A.R. No. B 20 of 1943-44.)
Siddāpuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the ruined temple of Aṅkālamma.

This is dated Śaka 1515, Vijaya, Śrāvaṇa śu. 5, Sunday, corresponding to 1593 A.D. July
22.

It registers the confirmation, by Siddha Bhikshāvṛitti-Ayya, of lands and incomes such as
Kāṇike, Kappam, Varttana etc. of the temples of Nāgavarapamma, Aṅkālamma and Puṭṭalamma
and also the right of supervision over the customary rites in these temples previously made to
Guḍivāriyam Channappa by Pedda-Ayya, the former Pontiff, for his having built the temples of
Aṅkālamma and later on renovated it and istalled therein a new image. When the temple was
desecrated and the image in it broken by the Muhammadans, he substituted new ones for the
old articles mudra gaḍa and pāvanālu taken away by the Muhammadans.

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(A.R. No. B 29 of 1943-44.)
Bairlūṭi Nandikottur Taluk, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Sculptured slab at the steps leading into the tank, southern side.

This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 9th century, on the basis of palaeography.
Damaged and worn out. It seems to refer to a certain Arjjuna etc.

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. B 30 of 1943-44.)
Anantapuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Sūryanārayaṇa temple.

This is dated Śaka 1535 Paridhāvi, (current), Srāvaṇa ba. 10, corresponding to 1612
A.D., August 11, Tuesday.
It states the setting of a Nandi-pillar by Chavaṭa Samgamayya son of Nagābattuḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(A.R. No. B 31 of 1943-44.)
Anantapuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up to the east of the Gangamma shrine.

This is an undated epigraph and may be assigned to the 15th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It records the construction of a temple in Anantapuram situated in the Mallikārjuni-
sīma and the consecration in it of the goddess Gaṅgadēvi by Gaṅgana, son of Abbana and
Gaṅgasāni.

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(A.R. No. B 32 of 1943-44.)
Anantapuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab lying in the field of Vaḍla Basapa.

This is dated Śaka 1090, the other details are lost. The corresponding date is 1168 A.D.
Much damaged. It refers to gifts of land. It also mentions Vīrayyaṅgāru.

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(A.R. No. B 33 of 1943-44.)
Dudyāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up on a platform under a tree near the Śiva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1489, Prabhava, Chaitra ba. 30, Wednesday, Solar Eclipse,
corresponding to 1567 A.D., April 9.

It records a gift of land as dasavanda mānya by Rājādhirāja Vasantarāya, son of
Yaṇṭagiri Vasantarāya to Veṅgaḷa-Reḍḍi, son of Timmā Reḍḍi for his having cleared the forest
and repaired the tank at Dudyāla in the Siddhāpura-sīma given by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Timmarājayyadēva-Mahārāja as nāyaṅkara to the donor with the stipulation that repairs to the
tank should be effected over year.

1489 ·agunēṭi prabhava saṁvatsara caitra ba 30 bu śrīmadrā-

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(A.R. No. B 34 of 1943-44.)
Nallakālava, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab kept behind the image of Vīrabhadra.

This is dated in the cyclic year Tāraṇa and other details are lost. However on the basis
of palaeography, it may be assigned to the 16th century A.D.

Damaged. It mentions mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju Timmarājayyadēva Mahārāju and
seems to record a gift of land made to Nandēla Viṭhalayaṁgāru.

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(A.R. No. B 35 of 1943-44.)
Nallakālava, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab at the doorway in front of the Vīrabhadra temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Vikāri, Pushya, ba. 3. On the basis of palaeography, it
may be assigned to the 16th century A.D.

It refers to an edurugaḍa executed by Yeṟṟabhaṭlu Mallubhaṭlu in favour of Taṁbaḷa
Jakkaya cancelling the original bond lost by the former, as the loan of half a Varāha advance
by him had been fully paid by the latter.

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(A.R. No. B 36 of 1943-44.)
Bhānumukkala, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Kēśva temple.
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1491, Māgha, Śu. 15 (= A.D., 1570.)

Damaged. It records the grant of the Vipravinōdi-Varttana made by a Vipravinōdin
(name lost) for the merit of his father Govindaya, for the amṛitapaḍi service to god.

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(A.R. No. B 37 of 1943-44.)
Vēṁpeṇṭa, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1651 (current) Saumya, Phāḷguṇa śu. 10, corresponding to 1730 A.D.
February 10, Monday.

It mentions the construction of a temple to god Śiva and consecration of Umā and
Mahēśvara therein by Channamarāju, son of Yeṟṟamarāju and grandson of Jūlakaṁṭi
Chennamarāju.

l.te: svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śaka va¨rṣaṁbulu 1651 ·agu-
nēṭi saumyanāma saṁvvatsara phālguṇa śu 10 lu || śrīmatu [veṁpeṭi]

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(A.R. No. B 38 of 1943-44.)
Vēṁpeṇṭa, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab near the well at Nāgulakaṭṭa.

This is dated Śaka 1786, Parābhava, Vaiśākha śu. 15. However, the cyclic year falls in
the Śaka year 1789, in which case the details of the date may correspond to 1867 A.D. May 18,
Saturday.

It records the gift of a well by Vīri-śeṭṭi, son of Mali-śeṭṭi and grandson of Gāṇḍla
Vīri-śeṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(A.R. No. B 39 of 1943-44.)
Abdullāpuram, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the ruined temple in a field to the east of the village.

This is dated Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Kārttīka śu. 12, corresponding to 1555 A.D.,
October 27, Sunday.

It registers the gift of the village Kōna samudram in Siddhāpuram-sīma by Veṅgaḷrāju,
son of Peddirāju and grandson of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Apratīkamalla Muṇḍlamalle Avubharāju
Dēvachōḍa-Mahārāju, of the solar race, Kāśyapa-gōtra Āśvalāyana-sūtra and Ṛikśākha for the
merit of his daughter Kōnama, for worship of god Tiruveṅgaḷanātha of Kōnasamudram.

inscription.head:
No. 538.
(A.R. No. B 40 of 1943-44.)
Bōyaṟēvula, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the compound of the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1482, Raudri, Āshāḍha śu. 12, corresponding to 1560 A.D., July 12,
Thursday.

It registers a gift of land made to Maṅgala Aṅkōju for performing the pipe and drum.
Service to god Tiruveṅgalanātha-dēva of Gurudūru by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Timmarājayyadēva
Mahārāju, son of Gaṅgarājayya and grandson of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Apratīkamalla Kuruchēṭi
Sōmarājayyadēva Chōḍa-Mahārāju of the solar race and Kāśyapa-gōtra. The gift lands are stated
to be situated in the village Bōyaṟēvula which was included in the Siddāpuraṁ-sīma which was
given to the donors as amara by Rāmarāju-Timma Rājayyadēva Mahārāju.

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(A.R. No. B 41 of 1943-44.)
Velpanūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab fixed in to the floor in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Aṅkalamma temple.

This is dated Śaka 1676, Bhava, Chaitra ba. 10, corresponding to 1754 A.D., April 17,
Wednesday.

Damaged. It seems to register a grant of money for worship of the goddess Aṅkāḷa
Paramēśvari by certain individuals.

l.te: va saṁvvatsara caitra ba 10 lu [||*] ve-
ṁ cci yiccina dharma śāsanaṁ yiṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(A.R. No. B 42 of 1943-44.)
Vēlpanūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab (another) fixed in to the floor in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Aṅkāḷamma temple.

This is dated Śaka 1608 (expired) Krōdhana, Āśvija śu. 12, corresponding to 1685 A.D.,
September 29, Tuesday.

It records a grant of land made by the reḍḍi and Karaṇam of the village to the stone
masons (Kāśa) Bālaya, Pāpaya and Koṇḍaya.

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(A.R. No. B 43 of 1943-44.)
Vēlpanūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab set up near the well in the compound of the Aṅkāḷamma temple.

This undated epigraph may be assingned to the 18th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

Damaged. It records the construction of a well provided with steps in front of the
temple of Aṅkāḷamma. It states that the inscription was incised by agasāli Śarabhaya of
Talamuḍipi.

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(A.R. No. B 44 of 1943-44.)
Vēlpanūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Thee fragments of slab lying in the Aṅkāḷamma temple.

This is dated Śaka 1240, Kāḷayukta, Āśvayuja śu. 11, Thursday, corresponding to 1318
A.D., October 6. However, the week day is Friday.

Fragmentary. It registers gifts of land at several places made by Mahāsāmanta Cheṟaku
Mahēśvara Reḍḍi who was ruling over Kaṁnāḍu for worship of god Mūlasthāna
Siddhanāthadēva of Vēlpunūru.

l.te: da padmōpajīviyaina svasti śrī manmahāsāmaṁta ce-
gnēya sthaḷamu • • • na mrōtukūri polamēranu rēgaḍu kha 2

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(A.R. No. B 45 of 1943-44.)
Vēlpanūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Fragment of a slab lying near the well in fron of the Śiva temple to the north of the
Aṅkāḷamma temple.

This is dated Śaka 1646, Śōbhakṛit, Śrāvaṇa śu. 7, Monday. The details of the date
are wrong.

Fragmentary. It seems to state that upto the date specified certain renovations (details lost)
were carried out near the shrines of Vīrēśvara and Siddhanāthadēva of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(A.R. No. B 46 of 1943-44.)
Rēgaḍigūḍru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the well called gāḍibhāvi, to the north of the village.

This is dated Śaka 1702 Śārvari, Jyēshṭha śu. 5, A.H. 1192, corresponding to 1780 A.D.,
August 5, Saturday.

It registers a grant of land situated in the Jāgīr of Hazarat Ummar Sha made by
Mirja Mahamad Bēku, to Pagiḍēla Kṛishnamma for filling with water the trough constructed by
the former.

l.te: svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śaka varuṣaṁ-
jēṣṭha śu 5 lu [||*] mi¨rjaphakurullā bēku komāru-
ki nīllu tōlēṭaṁduku pagiḍēla kṛṣṇammaku vicci-

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(A.R. No. B 47 of 1943-44.)
Rēgaḍigūḍru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Slab set up side of the water through near the well called gāḍibhāvi, to the north of
the village.

This is dated Śaka 1787, Krōdhana, Māgha ba 5, corresponding to 1866 A.D., February,
4 Sunday.

It records that the water trough which had been damaged was renovated and the lands
formerly granted were restored to Maddilēṭi, the grandson of Pagiḍēla Kṛishnamma for filling
the trough with water.

l.te: svasti śrī vijeyābhyudaya śālivāhana śaka varṣaṁbulu
1787 ·agunēṭi krōdhana nāma saṁ || māgha ba 5 lu lōgā vuṁnna

lēṭiki naḍustū vuṁnnadi yiṁduku yavaru ·ālayamainā kāśi

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(A.R. No. B 48 of 1943-44.)
Maddūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Base of the dvārapāla image (left side) in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is an undated record and may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states that the images of dvārapālakas were caused to be made by Anumanabōyuḍu
son of Nāgana bōyuḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(A.R. No. B 49 of 1943-44.)
Maddūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Base of the dvārapāla image (right side) in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is an undated record and may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states that the images of dvārapālakas were caused to be made by Anumanabōyuḍu
son of Nāgana bōyuḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(A.R. No. B 50 of 1943-44.)
Vāṇāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Kēśava temple.

This is an undated and record may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It records a gift of land sowable with 2 tūmūs of seed as Vṛitti to god Vīrabhadradēva
of Alaṁpur.

inscription.head:
No. 549.
(A.R. No. B 51 of 1943-44.)
Vāṇāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar set up in the temple of Gaṇēśa.

This is dated Śaka 1438, Āshāḍha ba. 5, corresponding to 1516 A.D., June 24 Tuesday.
In this record the cyclic year is damaged. The Śaka year 1438 falls in Dhātri.

Damaged. It seems to refer to a gift for the merit of Siddha Bhikshā vṛitti-Ayyaṅgāru
by his agent.

inscription.head:
No. 550.
(A.R. No. B 52 of 1943-44.)
Miṭṭa Kandāla, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar set up in fron of the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1275, Ānanda, (current), Kārttīka śu. 1, Thursday, corresponding to
1354 A.D. However, the week day is Saturday.

It registers the grant of the village in Kaṁnāḍu for worship of the god
Saṅgamēśvaradēva to the religious teacher Aghōraśivāchārya, by Tipparāju son of Saṁgarāju.
Further it also mentions about the erection of Nandi-staṁbha.

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(A.R. No. B 53 of 1943-44.)
Rāmasamudram, hamlet of Tarigoppula, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of a ruined well in the field on the way to Miṭṭa Kondāla.
This is dated Śaka 1649, corresponding to 1727 A.D.
Incomplete. The inscription stops abruptly after mentioning the date.

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(A.R. No. B 54 of 1943-44.)
Lingāla, hamlet of Jūṭūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Fragment of slab lying in the temple of Vināyaka.

This is an undated epigraph and may be assigned to the 14th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
Fragmentary. It gives the epithet of a chief, name lost.

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(A.R. No. B 55 of 1943-44.)
Lingāla, hamlet of Jūṭūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar in the field known as basavannapāḍu on the way to Jūṭūru from Lingāla.

This is dated Śaka 1214 Nandana, Phālguṇa śu. 15, Sunday corresponding to 1293 A.D.,
February 22.

It registers grant of lands made by Rācha Rudradēva, son of Mahāsāmanta Cheṟaku
Vēlūri Bollaya Reḍḍi to god Mallikārjuna for the Paṁchamṛita-mahāpūja and mahānaivēdya and
worship of the god and for the maintaining the (sattras) charity homes on the occasion of the
former's visit to Śriśailam for the Vasanta-mahōtsava of the god. The gift lands which were
situated in Kaṁnāḍu were made over to the Kalmaṭha and entrusted to Paramaśivāchārya, the
pontiff of the maṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 554.
(A.R. No. B 56 of 1943-44.)
Jūṭūru, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab lying in the temple of Mallidēva.

This is an undated record and may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
Fragmentary. It refers to a gift of land as vṛitti made to a god (name lost).

l.te: • • • du yedumu dēvara • • ṁ naku vritti viḍcitimi [yiṁ]-
[dku] yevvaru • • nānu gaṁga karuta kavila campina pāpāna

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(A.R. No. B 57 of 1943-44.)
Tarigoppula, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near a well adjacent to the Śiva temple on the bank of a canal.

This is dated Śaka 1567, Pārthiva, Śrāvaṇa ba. 5, corresponding to 1645 A.D., August
5, Saturday.

It mentions the local officer Nāru Abdula Vahābā Sāhebu and states that the well was
constructed by Sūrpa (probably Sūrappa) son of Kōnpa (Kōnappa) of Tarigōpula.

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(A.R. No. B 58 of 1943-44.)
Tarigoppula, Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab fixed into the floor in the maṇḍapa of the Vīrabhadra temple.

This is dated in cyclic year Hēmalambi, Aśvayuja ba. 2, Tuesday. However, on the
basis of palaeography, it may be assigned to the 16th century A.D.

It registers a gift of the money income of 3 māḍas by Chaṁnaya and another (name
lost) for worship of the gods Perumāḷ and Vīrēśvara of the village.

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(A.R. No. B 59 of 1943-44.)
Lingāpuram, hamlet of Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Capital of a curved wooden Pāru column in the Chennakēśava manchāla temple.

This is dated Śaka 1667, Krōdhana, Vaiśākha ba. 10, corresponding to 1745 A.D., May
15, Wednesday.

It states that the temple and the carvings depicting scenes from Rāmāyaṇa (on the
wooden column) were completed on the date specified.

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(A.R. No. B 60 of 1943-44.)
Lingāpuram, hamlet of Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa on the way to the temple of the goddess (Aṁbā).

This undated epigraph may be assigned to the 14-15th centuries, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states that the gōpura was the gift of Vidyādhara Mahāpātra, the devoted servant of
god Mallēśvara of Śrīparvata, and that it was constructed under the supervision of Dōmāyidēva
by the artisan Nāgaramahārāṇa.

l.te: da dēvu taṁḍri ravidēvu taṁḍri haridēvu [vī]-

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(A.R. No. B 62 of 1943-44.)
Śriśailam, hamlet of Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: broken slab fixed into the floor near the gateway of the shrine of the goddess (Aṁba).

This damaged epigraph may be assigned to the 17th century, on the basis of
palaeography.
Damaged. It seems to mention certain individual named Mēḍā Guraṁmma.

inscription.head:
No. 560.
(A.R. No. B 64 of 1943-44.)
Isakāla, hamlet of Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab near the temple of Śiva on the way to Dudyāla.

This is dated Śaka 1486, Raktākshi, Māgha śu. 15, corresponding to 1565 A.D., January
16, Tuesday.

It records the grant of land at Isukāla as mānya by Rēmidēvuni Virūpayyaṁgāru of
Nāgalūṭi to the artisans China-Vīrōju and Tarigoppula Pōchamōju for their having brought the
images meant for processions for the temple of Kēśava at the village.

l.note: tra is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(A.R. No. B 77 of 1943-44.)
Dūpāḍu, Markapur Taluk, Prakasam District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near a well in the shephered's street.

This is dated only in the cyclic year Krōdhi Nija Āshāḍha śu. 15, Friday. However,
on the basis of palaeography, it may be assigned to the 18th century A.D.

It states that the step-well was constructed by the merchants (Nagarasvāmis). It also mentions
Nagarēśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(A.R. No. B 108 of 1943-44.)
Tiruvēṇḍipuram, Cuddalore Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: Stone fixed in the old Kuḍiraivāhana maṇḍapa in the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 18th century, on the basis of
palaeography.

It states that the maṇḍapa was constructed by Nāraṇappa Nāyudu, son of Appayya-
Nāyuḍu who was the son of Kaḍiyam Sūrappa-Nāyaḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(A.R. No. B 135 of 1943-44.)
Pedda-Nāgapūṇḍi, Chandragiri Taluk, Chittoor District.
inscription.p: West wall of central shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1638, Drumukhi, Āsvīja śu. 10, corresponding to 1716 A.D.,
September 15, Saturday.

It registers gift of six sites in the agrahāram Peda-Nāgapūḍi by Chilaṁpāṭi
Akkappanāyani [Rū] Pālanāyini Nāgapa.

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(A.R. No. B 141 of 1943-44.)
Santāna Vēṇugōpālapuram, Chandragiri Taluk, Chittoor District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vēṇugōpalasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1678, Dhātu, Āshāḍha ba. 5, Friday, corresponding to 1756 A.D.,
July 16.

It records gift of land by Kaṭhāri Sāḷuva Mākarāju-Bommarāju-dēva Mahārāju for
providing milk and curds to the temple of Santānagōpālasvāmi. It further states that the donee
was permitted to graze his cattle on the temple land.

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(A.R. No. B 142 of 1943-44.)
Nochchili, Chandragiri Taluk, Chittoor District.
inscription.p: Base of the flagstaff in the Gaṅgadharēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1804 Chitrabhānu, Māgha śu. 2, Friday, corresponding to 1883 A.D.
It states that the flag-staff was set up on the date specified.

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(A.R. No. B 152 of 1943-44.)
Poddaṭṭūrupēṭa, Tiruttani Taluk, Tiruttani District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: East wall of the Vēṇugōpalasvāmi temple.
This is dated Śaka 1478, Nala, Śrāvaṇa śu. 11, Saturday, corresponding to 1556 A.D., July 18.

Damaged. It seems to record a grant to different Brāhmaṇas by Chinna Timmayadēva-
Mahārāya, son of Rāmarāju and grandson of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Āravēṭi Viṭṭhalarāja-Rāmarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(A.R. No. B 206 of 1943-44.)
Kuḻittalai, Kulittalai Taluk, Trichinapally District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: South wall of maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the dilapi dated Śiva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1685, Svabhānu, Mārgaśira śu. 7, Sunday corresponding to 1763
A.D., December 11.

It records the installation of a liṅga in Chokkanāthapura-agrahāra by a certain
Chinnasāmi Cheṭṭi.
